revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hyosu-jinja (Nishiwaki)]][[ja:兵主神社 (西脇市)]] {{Expand Japanese|兵主神社 (西脇市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hyōsu Shrine | native_name = 兵主神社 | image = Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hyōsu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.02485|134.993959}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|ca|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|es|Ōkuninushi|fa|اوکونینوشی|fr|Ōkuninushi|hu|Ókuninusi|id|Ookuninushi|it|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|ko|오오쿠니누시|mni|ꯑꯣꯀꯨꯅꯤꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Ōkuninushi|pt|Ookuninushi|ru|Окунинуси|simple|Ōkuninushi|sr|Okuninuši|sv|Ōkuninushi|tr|Ōkuninushi|uk|Окунінуші|vi|Ōkuninushi|zh|大國主|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hyōsu Shrine'''|兵主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135195551|ltq=Q135195551|qid=Q135195551|1=Hyōzu shrine}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498723|ltq=Q11498723|qid=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} ** Series ordinal: 35 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041167|ltq=Q135041167|qid=Q135041167|1=Aratano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Konawino Shrine|lt=Konawino Shrine|qid=Q135041168|1=Konawino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q869020|ltq=Q869020|qid=Q869020|1=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Q1047523|ltq=Q1047523|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Taka District, Hyōgo|lt=Taka district|en|Taka District, Hyōgo|es|Distrito de Taka|fr|District de Taka|id|Distrik Taka, Hyōgo|it|Distretto di Taka|ja|多可郡|ko|다카군|pl|Powiat Taka|zh|多可郡|qid=Q1042243|1=Taka District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.02485|134.993959}} == image (P18) == * Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q276944|ltq=Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183041 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひょうすじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005017085 == Commons category (P373) == * Hyosu-jinja (Nishiwaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県西脇市黒田庄町372-2', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県西脇市黒田庄町岡372', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hyosu-jinja (Nishiwaki)}} [[JA:兵主神社 (西脇市)]] {{wikidata link|Q28684010}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hyōsu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡.jpg|270px]]
2011 autumn festival and shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishiwaki|Q869020|qid=Q869020|1=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kurodashō-chō|Q6446288|qid=Q6446288|1=Kurodashō, Hyōgo}} Oka 372-2 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ashihara-no-Shikoo|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}
{{ill|lt=Yashimajinumi|Q55522907|qid=Q55522907|lt=Suga-no-Yuyamanushi-Minasaruhiko-Yashimashino-no-Mikoto|1=Yashimajinumi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=782|Q30550|qid=Q30550|1=782}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 1) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = Hyōsu-san (common name) |札所等 = |例祭 = Second Sunday of October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Hyōsu Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hyōsu Shrine''' (ひょうすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kurodashō-chō|Q6446288|qid=Q6446288|1=Kurodashō, Hyōgo}} Oka, {{ill|lt=Nishiwaki|Q869020|qid=Q869020|1=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} under the old shrine ranking system. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Ōshi-gō area{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nishiwaki.lg.jp/kakukanogoannai/soumubu/hisyokouhouka/furusato/1435046702825.html|title=Hyōsu Shrine (Kurodashō-chō Oka)|publisher=Nishiwaki City|date=2015-06-23|accessdate=2020-12-13}}, and is locally known as "Hyōsu-san"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nishiwaki-kanko.jp/guide/history/hyosujinja.html|title=Hyōsu Shrine|publisher=Nishiwaki City Tourism Association|accessdate=2020-12-13}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおなむちのみこと) ; Associate deities *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (やちほこのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ashihara-no-Shikoo|1=Ōkuninushi}} (あしはらのしこお) *{{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} (おおものぬしのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Yashimajinumi|Q55522907|qid=Q55522907|lt=Suga-no-Yuyamanushi-Minasaruhiko-Yashimashino-no-Mikoto|1=Yashimajinumi}} (すがのゆやまぬしみなさるひこやしましのみこと) === History === The Hyōsu deity derives from the {{ill|lt=Han dynasty|Q7209|qid=Q7209|1=Han dynasty}}'s {{ill|lt=Liu Bang|Q7210|qid=Q7210|lt=Emperor Gaozu|1=Emperor Gaozu of Han}} enshrining "{{ill|lt=Chiyou|Q1147255|qid=Q1147255|1=Chiyou}}" (Shiyū) and praying for victory. According to {{ill|lt=Kaizuka Shigeki|Q11634459|qid=Q11634459|1=Shigeki Kaizuka}}, there is also the view that the deity was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=blacksmith deity|1=UNKNOWN}} — a {{ill|lt=god|Q178885|qid=Q178885|1=Deity}} of {{ill|lt=weapon|Q728|qid=Q728|1=Weapon}} manufacture. According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=782|Q30550|qid=Q30550|1=782}}), Okamoto Shuri-no-Taibu, who had been dispatched from {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} to the {{ill|lt=provincial office|Q11421342|qid=Q11421342|1=kokuga}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, cleared forest and built the shrine buildings. Two years later, in Enryaku 3 ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|qid=Q30083|1=784}}), the {{ill|lt=Hyōsu deity of Yamato Anashi|Q11597229|qid=Q11597229|lt=Hyōsu deity of Yamato Anashi|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine}} was invited (kanjō). According to 'Nihon no Kamigami 2,' '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto Bunshin Ruishashō|1=UNKNOWN}}' records "Left seat: Yachihoko-no-Mikoto, also called Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto, divine body is a broad spear, Hyōsu Izuhoko deity is this," from which it is inferred that by the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} the Hyōsu deity was associated with Yachihoko-no-Mikoto[http://kamnavi.jp/ym/hiboko/kuroda.htm Kamnavi — Hyōsu Shrine (Kurodashō-chō)] accessed February 26, 2019. === Precinct === === Architecture === The worship hall is a rare nationally notable example of thatched-roof ({{ill|lt=kayabuki|Q874531|qid=Q874531|1=Thatching}}) {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} in the long-floor style with {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} architectural features. According to the {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}}, it was renovated in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|qid=Q6766|1=1591}}). During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} attacked {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|qid=Q11242473|1=Miki Castle}} ({{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|qid=Q837435|1=Bessho Nagaharu}}), his retainer {{ill|lt=Kuroda Yoshitaka|Q907019|qid=Q907019|1=Kuroda Yoshitaka}} donated rice-field income for victory prayers, and the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} is said to have been renovated with those donated funds. In March {{ill|lt=1977|Q2481|qid=Q2481|1=1977}} (Shōwa 52), it was designated as an important cultural property of Hyōgo Prefecture. === Festivals === Every October, the annual {{ill|lt=autumn festival|Q11595783|qid=Q11595783|1=Autumn Matsuri}} is held. Five futon-yatai floats are paraded from the Ōbushi, Kita, Daimon, Tsumai, and Oka districts, and traditional events such as "Rekkei," which imitates a {{ill|lt=daimyō procession|Q11433354|qid=Q11433354|1=daimyō gyōretsu}}, are performed with great splendor. The floats enter the shrine precincts from around 11 AM on the main day. After the floats are set up in the precincts, children's dedicatory sumo is held; after mochi-throwing concludes around 2 PM, the floats depart. The festival was originally held on October 14 and 15, but the float procession and other festivities are now held on the second Saturday and Sunday of October{{Cite web|和書|url=http://prosv8.tok2.com/~harima/nisiwaki/hyousu.htm|title=Hyōsu Shrine Autumn Festival Introduction|accessdate=2018-10-7}}. Additionally, in {{ill|lt=1983|Q2434|qid=Q2434|1=1983}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 58), a grand festival commemorating the 1,200th anniversary of the shrine's founding (kanjō) was held. File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）2011.10.9 DSCF1058.jpg|In the precinct, bearers demonstrate the bold technique of rotating the floats by cradling the carrying poles. File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）2011.10.9 DSCF1192.jpg|Annual festival futon-yatai float (October 9, 2011) File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）2011.10.9 DSCF1105.jpg|Annual festival futon-yatai float (October 9, 2011) File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）2011.10.9 DSCF1082.jpg|Mikoshi before the torii (October 9, 2011) File:Hyozu-jinja 兵主神社例祭（西脇市黒田庄町岡）2011.10.9 DSCF1121.jpg|Many local children participate in the festival, and children's dedicatory sumo is held. === Visitor information === ; Address *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishiwaki|Q869020|qid=Q869020|1=Nishiwaki, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Kurodashō-chō|Q6446288|qid=Q6446288|1=Kurodashō, Hyōgo}} 372-2 ; Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Kakogawa Line|Q874467|qid=Q874467|1=Kakogawa Line}} {{ill|lt=Kurodashō Station|Q3096707|qid=Q3096707|1=Kurodashō Station}}, then approx. 5-minute walk === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Hyōsu Shrine|Q11392794|qid=Q11392794|1=Hyōzu Shrine (disambiguation)}} (disambiguation) * {{ill|lt=Hyōsu Taisha|Q11392795|qid=Q11392795|1=Hyōzu Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Anashi-ni-imasu-Hyōsu Shrine|Q11597229|qid=Q11597229|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Hyozu Shrine Annual Festival 2011}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6312117.html Hyōsu Shrine — Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Office] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hyōsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hyōsu Shrine]][[Category:Hyōzu shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:西脇市の建築物]] [[Category:西脇市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
